global.title: GraphQL and Schema

coreIdeas.contextPhrase: GraphQL serves as the lingua franca across the entire Powerhouse CQRS architecture — defining write-side schemas, read-side query interfaces, and the contract between processors and consumers — making it the single language every developer must know.

Custom scalars paired with Zod validation schemas bridge the gap between GraphQL type definitions and runtime safety, ensuring that values like Ethereum addresses and currency amounts are validated both at the schema boundary and deep within business logic.

The Powerhouse design system defines GraphQL scalars that serve double duty as both type-system extensions and the source of truth for UI component generation, ensuring visual consistency across all editors that render the same data types.

The component library generated from GraphQL scalars provides ready-made input components for every custom type, so editor developers get validated, accessible form fields without building them from scratch for each new document model.

The node actions hook connects drive-level operations to the GraphQL type system, enabling editors to dispatch structural drive mutations like creating folders, moving documents, and managing permissions through the same typed, validated pipeline used for document content operations.
Comprehensive schema design conventions — type naming, arrays, optional fields, operation patterns
Schema optionality pattern: fields must be optional to support empty document creation
Mandatory array notation requirement learned from service-offering schema — silent failures otherwise
Input schema philosophy: simple specific fields reflecting user intent, not internal state shape
TypeScript naming gotcha: code generator expects XState not XGlobalState — mismatch breaks generation
InputMaybe vs Maybe type mismatch requires explicit coalescing in reducer code

global.description: GraphQL as the universal schema language for Powerhouse: custom scalars, Zod validation, the design system component library, and drive operation hooks.
global.orientation: GraphQL is not just a query language in Powerhouse — it is the lingua franca that connects every layer of the platform. Document model schemas are defined in GraphQL SDL. Custom scalars extend the type system with domain-specific types like Currency, EthereumAddress, and OID, each backed by Zod validation to ensure runtime type safety matches compile-time guarantees. The Powerhouse design system translates these GraphQL scalars into a reusable component library, so that every scalar type has a corresponding UI input component that handles formatting, validation, and accessibility. Even drive-level operations flow through a GraphQL-typed system via the node actions hook. And in the CQRS read side, GraphQL serves as the query interface for subgraphs and processors. This MOC connects the schema primitives to their downstream consumers across the entire stack.
